title: RNC chair Lara Trump criticizes GOP candidate over comments on Trump verdict

title: Netanyahu aide says Biden's Gaza plan isn't 'a good deal,' but Israel accepts it

title: Hunter Biden trial: Why his gun case hinges on one fateful drug-free day
shortDescription: Hunter Biden's federal gun case may hinge on the legal interpretation of the word 'addict' and whether he used drugs on a specific day in 2018.

title: Will Donald Trump use the Justice Department to go after rivals? He won't say.
shortDescription: In a Fox News interview, Trump also suggested without evidence that Americans would revolt if he is put in jail or placed under house arrest.

title: Hunter Biden's trials may include ex-wife's lawsuit over alimony
shortDescription: The son of President Joe Biden is scheduled to begin trial Monday on federal gun charges; a tax case is scheduled to start on Sept. 5.

title: White House Grandma: Obama family pays tribute to Marian Robinson
shortDescription: Marian Robinson kept a low profile as she helped care for the Obama family and their daughters during their time in the White House.

title: After verdict, poll shows some Republicans less likely to back Trump

title: Donald Trump says he raised nearly $53 million after his conviction

title: Voters question Hunter Biden trials as President stands by his son
shortDescription: As President Joe Biden gears up for the November election, he must first make his way through his son's criminal trials in Delaware and California.

title: Donald Trump's conviction has landmark impact on American history
shortDescription: Trump is the first former U.S. president convicted of a crime. Here's what the verdict in his New York trial means for his legacy and U.S. history.

title: Trump's state verdict can't be pardoned by Biden | Fact check
shortDescription: A president only has the constitutional power to pardon a federal offense. Trump's verdict in the 34-count indictment is at the New York state level.

title: Trump campaign warns Virginia's Rep. Bob Good not to use
shortDescription: "It is an abuse of voters’ trust": the former president's campaign warns Rep. Bob Good of Virginia not to use his name to get re-elected

title: Post overreaches by claiming Trump can't vote in 2024 | Fact check
shortDescription: Trump's conviction on felony charges in New York does not necessarily make him ineligible to vote in 2024, legal experts said.

title: Biden speaks on potential cease-fire in Gaza
shortDescription: President Joe Biden said a new agreement for a potential ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war includes the release of all hostages in three phases.

title: Trump conviction: Here's what's true and not | Fact check roundup
shortDescription: We break down what Trump and social media users got wrong about his sentence, ability to vote, jury instructions and a widespread reaction video.

title: Trump may be first convicted US president, but it's not a global first
shortDescription: More than a half-dozen democracies scattered around the globe have convicted their ex-leaders, including France, Israel and Italy.

title: Supposed Trump conviction celebration video is a meme | Fact check
shortDescription: The original video dates back to 2016 and shows a bar crowd in England cheering as the country scored a last-minute goal against Wales in Euro 2016.

title: Wyoming politicians condemn Trump trial results

title: Israel leader Benjamin Netanyahu invited to speak to Congress
shortDescription: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y., supported the invite despite previously criticizing Netanyahu's government.

title: Trump was convicted, but Stormy Daniels still owes him legal fees
shortDescription: Porn star Stormy Daniels - who is at the heart of the case in the hush money trial - still owes Trump more than half a million dollars in legal fees.

title: How Stormy Daniels reacted to the Trump guilty verdict
shortDescription: Stormy Daniels isn't talking yet about Trump's hush money conviction, but her lawyer relayed her reaction to the historic event and her role in it

title: Biden speaks on Trump's hush money conviction
shortDescription: Before delivering remarks on tensions in the Middle East, President Bidens spoke on former President Trump's conviction in the hush money trial in New York.

title: Trump falsely claims he faces up to 187 years in prison | Fact check
shortDescription: Each of Trump's 34 convictions has a maximum of four years imprisonment individually, but New York caps Class E felony sentences at 20 years.
